issues.body: The added test commentary calls the path/root equality "the soundness tie between a real member's proof and the RP's trust anchor" without stating that the ZK construction remains research-grade and is not externally audited pending sq-qhy4. On this ZK surface, the repository's live claims gate treats an uncaveated soundness claim as dishonest even when the narrower Merkle equality tested here is correct. In addition, research/zk-membership-pok-reaudit.md section 2 still records KeySet::hidden_issuer_root as calling issuer::key_set_root, so the load-bearing audit trail is stale after this change.
issues.fix_hint: Rephrase the comment as the narrower, demonstrated Merkle root/path consistency property, or add the required research-grade/not-externally-audited (sq-qhy4) caveat. Update the hidden-issuer root section of research/zk-membership-pok-reaudit.md in the same PR to name key_set_root_sparse and record that it is bit-identical to the dense construction.
issues.title: New hidden-issuer soundness wording is not caveated

verdict.summary: The sparse-root substitution and its regression test appear value-preserving and exercise the real KeySet path, including a deep authentication-path refold. However, the new ZK-facing comments introduce an uncaveated soundness claim, which violates the repository's live privacy/soundness-claims rule while external review sq-qhy4 remains pending. The load-bearing audit record also still names the dense builder and should be kept synchronized with this trust-anchor implementation change.
